creator: sweeney@addr.com
text:
OK, Gavin's mail prompted me to give this another go.  I was originally 
doing this with an internal ATA CDROM, I just tried again with an 
external USB CDROM and I get the following.  I am not convinced that 
this is the same error, I distinctly remember the "uptime: 1s" message 
before, now it doesn't get past the BTX loader:

Boot from CD/DVD : CD Loader 1.2

Building the boot loader arguments
Looking up /BOOT/LOADER... Found
Relocating the loader and the BTX
Starting the BTX loader

BTX loader 1.00  BTX version is 1.01
Consoles: internal video/keyboard

int=0000000d  err=00000000  efl=00030046  eip=0000460d
eax=00000400  ebx=00000700  ecx=00000000  edx=0000009f
esi=0000000c  edi=00000000  ebp=00000000  esp=000003fc
cs=f000  ds=43ae  es=4392    fs=0000  gs=0000  ss=9e3c
cs:eip=2e 0f 01 16 c8 46 0f 20-c0 0c 01 0f 22 c0 b8 20
       00 8e d8 0f 20 c0 24 fe-0f 22 c0 eb 00 66 58 c3
ss:esp=ae 43 00 00 7b 3d 01 4b-92 43 ae 43 e6 9b 47 91
       00 00 46 02 f8 47 09 00-38 04 00 00 9f 00 00 00
BTX halted
